meaning: n.结婚生活,婚姻n.[律]结婚的状态
examples: Like more and more women, she believes wedlock would cramp her style.
Instead, they raise children out of wedlock.
Some 70% of black babies are born out of wedlock.
Back in 1965, 25% of black children were born out of wedlock, nearly one-third fewer.
And there is, in fact, now high out-of-wedlock childbearing, almost 50 percent among Hispanics.
